description: Analysis of all lost leads across the 70-entry knowledge base. Identifies patterns in why leads were lost and what could have been done differently.

lost_leads.name: Boglarka
lost_leads.reason: Decided to sell property instead of STR. Could not make STR income compelling enough vs sale.

lost_leads.name: Ada
lost_leads.reason: R2R model — landlord consent not secured. Structurally incompatible.

lost_leads.name: Oliver
lost_leads.reason: Status-quo-resistant. Did not see compelling reason to change from selling.

lost_leads.name: Nina
lost_leads.reason: Purchase fell through or timing out. DLY-PUR risk materialised.

lost_leads.name: Gary
lost_leads.reason: Status-quo-resistant sell profile. Did not convert.

lost_leads.name: Shoaib
lost_leads.reason: Status-quo-resistant. No compelling trigger to change.

top_loss_reasons.note: R2R model fundamentally incompatible with Stayful. Cannot be converted without direct owner engagement.

top_loss_reasons.reason: Status quo preference with no trigger
top_loss_reasons.note: These leads needed a compelling income gap or a life event trigger. Quarterly re-engagement recommended.
SELL profile leads had 100% loss rate. Decision to sell driven by factors beyond Stayful's control.
PURCH lead whose transaction did not complete. Not controllable.
EX-STL or STL-SW who chose different agent. Requires stronger differentiation on personal service.

key_insights: R2R and SELL profiles are structurally high-loss — time investment should be minimal until structural blockers resolved
Status-quo-resistant leads need a life event trigger (tenant leaving, moving, financial pressure) — cannot be forced
Loss rate excluding R2R and SELL drops to approximately 6% — highly effective pipeline
Betrayal-damaged and certainty-seeker leads have 0% loss rate — patience pays off
PURCH leads need regular contact — purchase fall-through is the main risk
pipeline_health: Strong. 80% of leads remain active or nurture. Only 4 losses were potentially avoidable.
